questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在建立 REST API。公司對TLS的使用有嚴格的要求。該公司要求 API 端點使用 TLSv1.3。該公司還需要特定的公共第三方憑證授權單位 (CA) 來簽署 TLS 憑證。哪種解決方案可以滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company is creating a REST API. The company has strict requirements for the use of TLS. The company requires TLSv1.3 on the API endpoints. The company also requires a specific public third-party certificate authority (CA) to sign the TLS certificate. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 使用本機電腦建立由第三方簽署的憑證 C將憑證匯入至 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)。使用自訂網域在 Amazon API Gateway 中建立 HTTP API。配置自訂網域以使用憑證。
A.en: Use a local machine to create a certificate that is signed by the third-party CImport the certificate into AWS Certificate Manager (ACM). Create an HTTP API in Amazon API Gateway with a custom domain. Configure the custom domain to use the certificate.

B.zh: 在 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 中建立由第三方 CA 簽署的憑證。使用自訂網域在 Amazon API Gateway 中建立 HTTP API。配置自訂網域以使用憑證。
B.en: Create a certificate in AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) that is signed by the third-party CA. Create an HTTP API in Amazon API Gateway with a custom domain. Configure the custom domain to use the certificate.

C.zh: 使用 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 建立由第三方 CA 簽署的憑證。將憑證匯入 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)。使用 Lambda 函數 URL 建立 AWS Lambda 函數。設定 Lambda 函數 URL 以使用憑證。
C.en: Use AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) to create a certificate that is signed by the third-party CA. Import the certificate into AWS Certificate Manager (ACM). Create an AWS Lambda function with a Lambda function URL. Configure the Lambda function URL to use the certificate.

D.zh: 在 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 中建立由第三方 CA 簽署的憑證。使用 Lambda 函數 URL 建立 AWS Lambda 函數。設定 Lambda 函數 URL 以使用憑證。
D.en: Create a certificate in AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) that is signed by the third-party CA. Create an AWS Lambda function with a Lambda function URL. Configure the Lambda function URL to use the certificate.

A.zh: 正確。使用本機電腦建立由第三方 CA 簽署的憑證。將憑證匯入 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)。在 Amazon API Gateway 中使用自訂網域建立 HTTP API。配置自訂網域名稱以使用憑證。正確：AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 支援匯入第三方 CA 簽署的憑證。正確：API Gateway 支援透過自訂網域使用來自 ACM 的憑證。正確：可以透過API Gateway的自訂網域設定TLSv1.3。
A.en: Correct. Use the local machine to create a certificate signed by a third-party CA. Import the certificate into AWS Certificate Manager (ACM). Create an HTTP API with a custom domain name in Amazon API Gateway. Configure a custom domain name to use the certificate. Correct: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) supports importing certificates signed by third-party CAs. Correct: API Gateway supports using certificates from ACM via custom domain names. Correct: TLSv1.3 can be configured through API Gateway's custom domain name.

B.zh: 錯誤。在 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 中建立由第三方 CA 簽署的憑證。在 Amazon API Gateway 中使用自訂網域建立 HTTP API。配置自訂網域名稱以使用憑證。錯誤：AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 無法直接建立由第三方 CA 簽署的憑證。 ACM 只能建立由 Amazon 簽署的憑證。
B.en: Incorrect. Create a certificate signed by a third-party CA in AWS Certificate Manager (ACM). Create an HTTP API with a custom domain name in Amazon API Gateway. Configure a custom domain name to use the certificate. Error: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) cannot directly create certificates signed by third-party CAs. ACM can only create certificates signed by Amazon.

C.zh: 錯誤。此選項錯誤：Lambda 函數 URL 不支援自訂網域或 ACM 憑證配置。錯誤：Lambda 函數 URL 無法滿足 TLSv1.3 的嚴格要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Use AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) to create a certificate signed by a third-party CA. Import the certificate into AWS Certificate Manager (ACM). Create an AWS Lambda function with a Lambda function URL. Configure the Lambda function URL to use the certificate. Error: Lambda function URL does not support custom domain name or ACM certificate configuration. Error: Lambda function URL cannot meet the strict requirements of TLSv1.3.

D.zh: 錯誤。此選項錯誤：AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 無法直接建立由第三方 CA 簽署的憑證。錯誤：Lambda 函數 URL 不支援自訂網域或 ACM 憑證配置。
D.en: Incorrect. Create a certificate signed by a third-party CA in AWS Certificate Manager (ACM). Create an AWS Lambda function with a Lambda function URL. Configure the Lambda function URL to use the certificate. Error: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) cannot directly create certificates signed by third-party CAs. Error: Lambda function URL does not support custom domain name or ACM certificate configuration.

answer_text.zh: A. 使用本機電腦建立由第三方簽署的憑證 C將憑證匯入至 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)。使用自訂網域在 Amazon API Gateway 中建立 HTTP API。配置自訂網域以使用憑證。
answer_text.en: A. Use a local machine to create a certificate that is signed by the third-party CImport the certificate into AWS Certificate Manager (ACM). Create an HTTP API in Amazon API Gateway with a custom domain. Configure the custom domain to use the certificate.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (78%)； B (22%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：使用本機電腦建立由第三方 CA 簽署的憑證。將憑證匯入 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)。在 Amazon API Gateway 中使用自訂網域建立 HTTP API。配置自訂網域名稱以使用憑證。正確：AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 支援匯入第三方 CA 簽署的憑證。正確：API Gateway 支援透過自訂網域使用來自 ACM 的憑證。正確：可以透過API Gateway的自訂網域設定TLSv1.3。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (78%); B (22%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Use the local machine to create a certificate signed by a third-party CA. Import the certificate into AWS Certificate Manager (ACM). Create an HTTP API with a custom domain name in Amazon API Gateway. Configure a custom domain name to use the certificate. Correct: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) supports importing certificates signed by third-party CAs. Correct: API Gateway supports using certificates from ACM via custom domain names. Correct: TLSv1.3 can be configured through API Gateway's custom domain name.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在 AWS 上執行應用程式。應用程式收到的使用量不一致。該應用程式使用 AWS Direct Connect 連接到本機 MySQL 相容資料庫。本地資料庫始終使用至少 2 GiB 記憶體。該公司希望將本機資料庫遷移到託管 AWS 服務。該公司希望使用自動擴展功能來管理意外的工作負載增加。哪種解決方案能夠以最少的管理開銷來滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company runs an application on AWS. The application receives inconsistent amounts of usage. The application uses AWS Direct Connect to connect to an on-premises MySQL-compatible database. The on-premises database consistently uses a minimum of 2 GiB of memory. The company wants to migrate the on-premises database to a managed AWS service. The company wants to use auto scaling capabilities to manage unexpected workload increases. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST administrative overhead?

A.zh: 使用預設讀取和寫入容量設定預置 Amazon DynamoDB 資料庫。
A.en: Provision an Amazon DynamoDB database with default read and write capacity settings.

B.zh: 配置最小容量為 1 Aurora 容量單位 (ACU) 的 Amazon Aurora 資料庫。
B.en: Provision an Amazon Aurora database with a minimum capacity of 1 Aurora capacity unit (ACU).

C.zh: 配置最小容量為 1 Aurora 容量單位 (ACU) 的 Amazon Aurora Serverless v2 資料庫。
C.en: Provision an Amazon Aurora Serverless v2 database with a minimum capacity of 1 Aurora capacity unit (ACU).

D.zh: 預置具有 2 GiB 記憶體的 Amazon RDS for MySQL 資料庫。
D.en: Provision an Amazon RDS for MySQL database with 2 GiB of memory.

A.zh: 錯誤。DynamoDB是NoSQL資料庫，問題中明確提到需要遷移的資料庫是MySQL相容的資料庫，所以DynamoDB不符合要求。此外，DynamoDB 的預設讀寫容量設定可能不足以滿足應用程式的記憶體需求（2 GiB），且不支援直接遷移與 MySQL 相容的資料庫。
A.en: Incorrect. DynamoDB is a NoSQL database, and the question clearly mentions that the database that needs to be migrated is a MySQL-compatible database, so DynamoDB does not meet the requirements. Additionally, DynamoDB's default read and write capacity settings may not be sufficient for the application's memory requirements (2 GiB), and do not support direct migration of MySQL-compatible databases.

B.zh: 錯誤。雖然Aurora支援MySQL相容，但選項中並沒有提及Serverless功能，因為它沒有自動擴充功能。此外，固定容量的Aurora實例可能會導致資源消耗或無法應對突發流量。
B.en: Incorrect. Although Aurora supports MySQL compatibility, the serverless function is not mentioned in the options because it has no automatic expansion capabilities. In addition, fixed-capacity Aurora instances may cause resource consumption or have no way to deal with sudden traffic.

C.zh: 正確。Aurora Serverless v2 支援 MySQL 相容性，並具有自動擴充功能，可根據工作負載動態調整容量。最小容量設定為1 ACU，滿足最低記憶體需求（2 GiB），同時減少管理開銷。
C.en: Correct. Aurora Serverless v2 supports MySQL compatibility and has automatic expansion capabilities, which can dynamically adjust capacity according to workload. The minimum capacity is set to 1 ACU, which meets the minimum memory requirement (2 GiB) while reducing management overhead.

D.zh: 錯誤。RDS雖然支援MySQL相容，但它是固定容量的資料庫實例，不具備自動擴容功能。實例的大小和效能需要手動管理，增加了管理開銷。
D.en: Incorrect. Although RDS supports MySQL compatibility, it is a fixed-capacity database instance with no automatic expansion capabilities. The size and performance of instances need to be manually managed, increasing management overhead.

answer_text.zh: C. 配置最小容量為 1 Aurora 容量單位 (ACU) 的 Amazon Aurora Serverless v2 資料庫。
answer_text.en: C. Provision an Amazon Aurora Serverless v2 database with a minimum capacity of 1 Aurora capacity unit (ACU).

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：C (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：Aurora Serverless v2 支援 MySQL 相容性，並具有自動擴充功能，可根據工作負載動態調整容量。最小容量設定為1 ACU，滿足最低記憶體需求（2 GiB），同時減少管理開銷。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Aurora Serverless v2 supports MySQL compatibility and has automatic expansion capabilities, which can dynamically adjust capacity according to workload. The minimum capacity is set to 1 ACU, which meets the minimum memory requirement (2 GiB) while reducing management overhead.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望將事件驅動的程式設計模型與 AWS Lambda 結合使用。該公司希望減少在 Java 11 上運行的 Lambda 函數的啟動延遲。該公司對應用程式沒有嚴格的延遲要求。該公司希望在功能擴展時減少冷啟動和異常延遲。哪一種解決方案能夠最具成本效益地滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company wants to use an event-driven programming model with AWS Lambda. The company wants to reduce startup latency for Lambda functions that run on Java 11. The company does not have strict latency requirements for the applications. The company wants to reduce cold starts and outlier latencies when a function scales up. Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 設定 Lambda 預置並發。
A.en: Configure Lambda provisioned concurrency.

B.zh: 增加 Lambda 函數的逾時。
B.en: Increase the timeout of the Lambda functions.

C.zh: 增加 Lambda 函數的記憶體。
C.en: Increase the memory of the Lambda functions.

D.zh: 設定 Lambda SnapStart。
D.en: Configure Lambda SnapStart.

A.zh: 錯誤。設定Lambda預設並發 分析：預設並發可以減少冷啟動延遲，因為它會提前啟動一定數量的Lambda實例。但是，這種方法會增加成本，因為您需要為預先配置的並發實例付費。題目要求「性價比最高」，所以這個選項不符合要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Configure Lambda preset concurrency Analysis: Preset concurrency can reduce cold start delay because it will start a certain number of Lambda instances in advance. However, this approach increases costs because you need to pay for provisioned concurrent instances. The question requires "most cost-effective", so this option does not meet the requirements.

B.zh: 錯誤。增加Lambda函數的逾時時間分析：增加逾時時間並不會直接減少冷啟動延遲。超時僅影響函數運作的最大時間限制，與冷啟動無關。
B.en: Incorrect. Increase the timeout of the Lambda function Analysis: Increasing the timeout will not directly reduce the cold start delay. The timeout only affects the maximum time limit for the function to run and has nothing to do with cold start.

C.zh: 錯誤。增加Lambda函數的記憶體分析：增加記憶體可以提高函數的執行速度，但對冷啟動影響有限。冷啟動主要與初始化時間有關，而不是運行時效能。因此該選項不符合題目要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Increase the memory of the Lambda function Analysis: Increasing memory can improve the execution speed of the function, but has limited impact on cold start. Cold starts are primarily related to initialization time, not runtime performance. Therefore this option does not meet the question requirements.

D.zh: 正確。設定Lambda SnapStart分析：Lambda SnapStart是專為減少冷車啟動延遲而設計的函數，特別適合Java 11等語言。它透過在函數初始化後建立快照並重複使用快照來減少啟動時間。此選項滿足問題要求，是最具成本效益的解決方案。
D.en: Correct. Configuring Lambda SnapStart Analysis: Lambda SnapStart is a function specially designed to reduce cold start delay, especially suitable for languages ​​such as Java 11. It reduces startup time by creating snapshots after function initialization and reusing the snapshots. This option meets the question requirements and is the most cost-effective solution.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：D (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：設定Lambda SnapStart分析：Lambda SnapStart是專為減少冷車啟動延遲而設計的函數，特別適合Java 11等語言。它透過在函數初始化後建立快照並重複使用快照來減少啟動時間。此選項滿足問題要求，是最具成本效益的解決方案。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Configuring Lambda SnapStart Analysis: Lambda SnapStart is a function specially designed to reduce cold start delay, especially suitable for languages ​​such as Java 11. It reduces startup time by creating snapshots after function initialization and reusing the snapshots. This option meets the question requirements and is the most cost-effective solution.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家金融服務公司推出了一款使用 Amazon RDS for MySQL 資料庫的新應用程式。該公司使用該應用程式來追蹤股票市場趨勢。該公司每週只需要運行該應用程式 2 個小時。該公司需要優化運行資料庫的成本。哪一種解決方案能夠最具成本效益地滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A financial services company launched a new application that uses an Amazon RDS for MySQL database. The company uses the application to track stock market trends. The company needs to operate the application for only 2 hours at the end of each week. The company needs to optimize the cost of running the database. Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 將現有 RDS for MySQL 資料庫遷移到 Aurora Serverless v2 MySQL 資料庫叢集。
A.en: Migrate the existing RDS for MySQL database to an Aurora Serverless v2 MySQL database cluster.

B.zh: 將現有 RDS for MySQL 資料庫遷移到 Aurora MySQL 資料庫叢集。
B.en: Migrate the existing RDS for MySQL database to an Aurora MySQL database cluster.

C.zh: 將現有 RDS for MySQL 資料庫移轉到執行 MySQL 的 Amazon EC2 執行個體。為 EC2 執行個體購買執行個體預留。
C.en: Migrate the existing RDS for MySQL database to an Amazon EC2 instance that runs MySQL. Purchase an instance reservation for the EC2 instance.

D.zh: 將現有 RDS for MySQL 資料庫移轉到使用 MySQL 容器映像執行任務的 Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 叢集。
D.en: Migrate the existing RDS for MySQL database to an Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) cluster that uses MySQL container images to run tasks.

A.zh: 正確。Aurora Serverless v2 支援按需計費，可根據工作負載動態擴展和減少資源，非常適合間歇性工作負載（例如每週僅運行 2 小時）。成本最佳化：Aurora Serverless v2 僅在使用時計費，避免了閒置資源的成本。
A.en: Correct. Aurora Serverless v2 supports on-demand billing and can dynamically expand and reduce resources based on the workload, which is very suitable for intermittent workloads (such as only running for 2 hours a week). Cost optimization: Aurora Serverless v2 is only billed when used, avoiding the cost of idle resources.

B.zh: 錯誤。Aurora MySQL 資料庫叢集雖然效能較高，但它是連續運作且按需計費，無需條帶化。非條帶化工作負載需要進行成本最佳化。
B.en: Incorrect. Although the Aurora MySQL database cluster has higher performance, it runs continuously and is billed on demand without striping. There is a need for cost optimization for non-striped workloads.

C.zh: 錯誤。EC2 執行個體預留適合長期工作負載，而非間歇性工作負載。即使您預留了實例，您仍然會產生閒置資源的成本。
C.en: Incorrect. EC2 instance reservations are suitable for long-term workloads and are not intermittent workloads. Even if you reserve an instance, you still incur the cost of idle resources.

D.zh: 錯誤。雖然ECS可以運行容器化MySQL，但管理和配置的複雜性增加，並且在沒有條帶優化的情況下存根工作負載的成本增加。
D.en: Incorrect. Although ECS can run containerized MySQL, the complexity of management and configuration increases, and the cost of stubbing workloads without strip optimization.

answer_text.zh: A. 將現有 RDS for MySQL 資料庫遷移到 Aurora Serverless v2 MySQL 資料庫叢集。
answer_text.en: A. Migrate the existing RDS for MySQL database to an Aurora Serverless v2 MySQL database cluster.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (92%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：Aurora Serverless v2 支援按需計費，可根據工作負載動態擴展和減少資源，非常適合間歇性工作負載（例如每週僅運行 2 小時）。成本最佳化：Aurora Serverless v2 僅在使用時計費，避免了閒置資源的成本。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (92%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Aurora Serverless v2 supports on-demand billing and can dynamically expand and reduce resources based on the workload, which is very suitable for intermittent workloads (such as only running for 2 hours a week). Cost optimization: Aurora Serverless v2 is only billed when used, avoiding the cost of idle resources.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司將其應用程式部署在 AWS 區域中應用程式負載平衡器後面的 Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) 上。應用程式需要將資料儲存在 PostgreSQL 資料庫引擎中。該公司希望資料庫中的數據具有高可用性。該公司還需要增加讀取工作負載的能力。哪種解決方案能夠以最高的營運效率滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company deploys its applications on Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) behind an Application Load Balancer in an AWS Region. The application needs to store data in a PostgreSQL database engine. The company wants the data in the database to be highly available. The company also needs increased capacity for read workloads. Which solution will meet these requirements with the MOST operational efficiency?

A.zh: 建立配置全域資料表的 Amazon DynamoDB 資料庫表。
A.en: Create an Amazon DynamoDB database table configured with global tables.

B.zh: 建立具有多可用區部署的 Amazon RDS 資料庫。
B.en: Create an Amazon RDS database with Multi-AZ deployments.

C.zh: 建立具有多可用區資料庫叢集部署的 Amazon RDS 資料庫。
C.en: Create an Amazon RDS database with Multi-AZ DB cluster deployment.

D.zh: 建立配置跨區域唯讀副本的 Amazon RDS 資料庫。
D.en: Create an Amazon RDS database configured with cross-Region read replicas.

A.zh: 錯誤。DynamoDB是NoSQL資料庫，問題明確需要使用PostgreSQL資料庫引擎（關聯式資料庫）。雖然DynamoDB的全域表可以提供高可用性和跨區域的可擴充性，但它並不能滿足問題所要求的資料庫類型。
A.en: Incorrect. DynamoDB is a NoSQL database, and the question clearly requires the use of the PostgreSQL database engine (relational database). Although DynamoDB's global tables can provide high availability and cross-region scalability, it does not meet the type of database required by the question.

B.zh: 錯誤。部分正確：多可用區部署可以提供高可用性，但它主要用於故障轉移，而不是擴展讀取工作負載。此選項沒有提及唯讀副本，因此無法解決「增加讀取工作負載容量」的需求。
B.en: Incorrect. Partially correct: a Multi-AZ deployment can provide high availability, but it is primarily used for failover, not scaling read workloads. This option makes no mention of read replicas and therefore does not address the need to "increase read workload capacity".

C.zh: 正確。多可用區資料庫叢集部署不僅提供高可用性，還支援多個讀取節點，從而提高讀取工作負載的容量。這是滿足問題要求的最佳方案，運作效率高。
C.en: Correct. Multi-AZ DB cluster deployment not only provides high availability, but also supports multiple read nodes, thereby increasing the capacity of read workloads. This is the best solution to meet the requirements of the question and has high operational efficiency.

D.zh: 錯誤。跨區域只讀副本主要用於跨區域擴展，但問題中沒有提及跨區域要求。另外，跨區域唯讀副本的設定和管理較為複雜，運作效率較低。
D.en: Incorrect. Cross-region read replicas are mainly used for cross-region expansion, but the question does not mention cross-region requirements. In addition, the setup and management of cross-region read replicas are more complex and the operation efficiency is lower.

answer_text.zh: C. 建立具有多可用區資料庫叢集部署的 Amazon RDS 資料庫。
answer_text.en: C. Create an Amazon RDS database with Multi-AZ DB cluster deployment.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：C (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：多可用區資料庫叢集部署不僅提供高可用性，還支援多個讀取節點，從而提高讀取工作負載的容量。這是滿足問題要求的最佳方案，運作效率高。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Multi-AZ DB cluster deployment not only provides high availability, but also supports multiple read nodes, thereby increasing the capacity of read workloads. This is the best solution to meet the requirements of the question and has high operational efficiency.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在使用 Amazon API Gateway 和 AWS Lambda 在 AWS 上建立 RESTful 無伺服器 Web 應用程式。該 Web 應用程式的使用者將分佈在不同的地理位置，並且該公司希望減少這些使用者的 API 請求的延遲。解決方案架構師應該使用哪種類型的端點來滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company is building a RESTful serverless web application on AWS by using Amazon API Gateway and AWS Lambda. The users of this web application will be geographically distributed, and the company wants to reduce the latency of API requests to these users. Which type of endpoint should a solutions architect use to meet these requirements?

A.zh: 專用端點
A.en: Private endpoint

B.zh: 區域終點
B.en: Regional endpoint

C.zh: 介面 VPC 終端節點
C.en: Interface VPC endpoint

D.zh: 邊緣最佳化端點
D.en: Edge-optimized endpoint

A.zh: 錯誤。（私有端點）私有端點用於存取VPC內的API網關，適用於內部應用或安全性需求較高的場景。不適合地理分佈的用戶，因為它無法透過 CloudFront 優化延遲。錯誤原因：無法滿足全球用戶降低存取延遲的需求。
A.en: Incorrect. (Private endpoint) Private endpoint is used to access API Gateway within a VPC, suitable for internal applications or scenarios that require high security. Not suitable for geographically distributed users as it cannot optimize latency via CloudFront. Reason for the error: Unable to meet the need to reduce access delays for global users.

B.zh: 錯誤。（區域終端節點）區域終端節點直接在指定的 AWS 區域內提供 API 閘道服務。適合一個區域內的用戶，但對於地理位置分散的用戶來說，延遲可能會更高。錯誤原因：無法透過全球分佈的邊緣站點來優化延遲。
B.en: Incorrect. (Region Endpoint) A regional endpoint provides API Gateway services directly within a specified AWS region. Suitable for users within a region, but latency may be higher for users who are geographically dispersed. Error reason: Unable to optimize latency through globally distributed edge locations.

C.zh: 錯誤。（介面 VPC 終端節點）介面 VPC 終端節點用於透過 AWS PrivateLink 存取 VPC 內的服務。主要用於安全性和專網存取場景，與降低全域用戶延遲無關。錯誤原因：不適合優化全域使用者的存取延遲。
C.en: Incorrect. (Interface VPC Endpoint) The Interface VPC endpoint is used to access services within a VPC through AWS PrivateLink. Mainly used for security and private network access scenarios and has nothing to do with reducing global user latency. Reason for the error: Not suitable for optimizing access latency for global users.

D.zh: 正確。（邊緣最佳化終端節點）邊緣最佳化終端節點透過 Amazon CloudFront 將 API Gateway 流量分發到世界各地的邊緣站點。適合地理位置分散的用戶，可顯著降低延遲。滿足全球用戶降低訪問時延的需求。
D.en: Correct. (Edge Optimization Endpoint) Edge Optimization Endpoint distributes API Gateway traffic to edge locations around the world through Amazon CloudFront. Suitable for geographically distributed users, it can significantly reduce latency. Meet the need to reduce access latency for global users.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：D (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：（邊緣最佳化終端節點）邊緣最佳化終端節點透過 Amazon CloudFront 將 API Gateway 流量分發到世界各地的邊緣站點。適合地理位置分散的用戶，可顯著降低延遲。滿足全球用戶降低訪問時延的需求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: (Edge Optimization Endpoint) Edge Optimization Endpoint distributes API Gateway traffic to edge locations around the world through Amazon CloudFront. Suitable for geographically distributed users, it can significantly reduce latency. Meet the need to reduce access latency for global users.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司使用 Amazon CloudFront 發行版為其網站提供內容頁面。公司需要確保客戶在造訪公司網站時使用TLS憑證。該公司希望自動建立和更新 TLS 憑證。哪種解決方案能夠以最高的營運效率滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company uses an Amazon CloudFront distribution to serve content pages for its website. The company needs to ensure that clients use a TLS certificate when accessing the company's website. The company wants to automate the creation and renewal of the TLS certificates. Which solution will meet these requirements with the MOST operational efficiency?

A.zh: 使用 CloudFront 安全性原則建立憑證。
A.en: Use a CloudFront security policy to create a certificate.

B.zh: 使用 CloudFront 來源存取控制 (OAC) 建立憑證。
B.en: Use a CloudFront origin access control (OAC) to create a certificate.

C.zh: 使用 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 建立憑證。對網域使用 DNS 驗證。
C.en: Use AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) to create a certificate. Use DNS validation for the domain.

D.zh: 使用 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 建立憑證。對網域使用電子郵件驗證。
D.en: Use AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) to create a certificate. Use email validation for the domain.

A.zh: 錯誤。CloudFront 的安全性策略用於定義如何保護內容（例如 TLS 版本、加密演算法），但無法建立和管理 TLS 憑證。
A.en: Incorrect. CloudFront's security policy is used to define how to protect content (such as TLS version, encryption algorithm), but it cannot create and manage TLS certificates.

B.zh: 錯誤。CloudFront 的來源存取控制 (OAC) 用於限制對來源的訪問，與 TLS 憑證的建立和管理無關。
B.en: Incorrect. CloudFront's Origin Access Control (OAC) is used to restrict access to origins and has nothing to do with the creation and management of TLS certificates.

C.zh: 正確。AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 可以自動建立和續訂 TLS 憑證。 DNS 驗證是一種適用於自動化流程的高效驗證方法。
C.en: Correct. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) can automatically create and renew TLS certificates. DNS verification is an efficient verification method suitable for automated processes.

D.zh: 錯誤。雖然ACM支援電子郵件驗證，但電子郵件驗證需要人工幹預，無法自動化，運作效率較低。
D.en: Incorrect. Although ACM supports email verification, email verification requires manual intervention, cannot be automated, and the operation efficiency is low.

answer_text.zh: C. 使用 AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 建立憑證。對網域使用 DNS 驗證。
answer_text.en: C. Use AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) to create a certificate. Use DNS validation for the domain.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：C (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) 可以自動建立和續訂 TLS 憑證。 DNS 驗證是一種適用於自動化流程的高效驗證方法。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) can automatically create and renew TLS certificates. DNS verification is an efficient verification method suitable for automated processes.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司部署了一個使用 Amazon DynamoDB 作為資料庫層的無伺服器應用程式。該應用程式的用戶數量大幅增加。該公司希望將資料庫回應時間從毫秒提高到微秒，並快取對資料庫的請求。哪種解決方案能夠以最少的營運開銷滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company deployed a serverless application that uses Amazon DynamoDB as a database layer. The application has experienced a large increase in users. The company wants to improve database response time from milliseconds to microseconds and to cache requests to the database. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 使用 DynamoDB 加速器 (DAX)。
A.en: Use DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX).

B.zh: 將資料庫移轉到 Amazon Redshift。
B.en: Migrate the database to Amazon Redshift.

C.zh: 將資料庫移轉到 Amazon RDS。
C.en: Migrate the database to Amazon RDS.

D.zh: 使用 Amazon ElastiCache for Redis。
D.en: Use Amazon ElastiCache for Redis.

A.zh: 正確。DAX 是專為 DynamoDB 設計的記憶體快取服務，可將回應時間從毫秒縮短到微秒。它與 DynamoDB 無縫集成，並且由於它是託管服務，因此營運開銷非常低。
A.en: Correct. DAX is a memory caching service specially designed for DynamoDB, which can reduce response time from milliseconds to microseconds. It integrates seamlessly with DynamoDB and has very low operational overhead because it is a hosted service.

B.zh: 錯誤。Amazon Redshift 是一種資料倉儲服務，適合分析工作負載而非高頻事務工作負載。遷移到Redshift不僅不適合場景需求，而且還會增加營運開銷。
B.en: Incorrect. Amazon Redshift is a data warehouse service suitable for analytical workloads rather than high-frequency transactional workloads. Migrating to Redshift is not only unsuitable for scenario requirements, but also increases operational overhead.

C.zh: 錯誤。Amazon RDS 是一種關聯式資料庫服務。雖然它可以提供低延遲，但它不具備 DynamoDB 的 Serverless 功能。遷移到 RDS 會增加管理開銷，且不一定能實現微秒響應時間。
C.en: Incorrect. Amazon RDS is a relational database service. Although it can provide low latency, it does not have the serverless features of DynamoDB. Migrating to RDS increases management overhead and may not necessarily achieve microsecond response times.

D.zh: 錯誤。雖然 ElastiCache for Redis 可以提供低延遲緩存，但需要額外的開發工作才能整合到現有 DynamoDB 架構中。與DAX相比，操作開銷更高，且不像DAX和DynamoDB那樣緊密整合。
D.en: Incorrect. While ElastiCache for Redis can provide low-latency caching, it requires additional development work to integrate into the existing DynamoDB architecture. Compared with DAX, the operation overhead is higher, and it is not as tightly integrated as DAX and DynamoDB.

answer_text.zh: A. 使用 DynamoDB 加速器 (DAX)。

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (91%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：DAX 是專為 DynamoDB 設計的記憶體快取服務，可將回應時間從毫秒縮短到微秒。它與 DynamoDB 無縫集成，並且由於它是託管服務，因此營運開銷非常低。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (91%). Popular discussion and key point summary: DAX is a memory caching service specially designed for DynamoDB, which can reduce response time from milliseconds to microseconds. It integrates seamlessly with DynamoDB and has very low operational overhead because it is a hosted service.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司運行一個使用 Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL 的應用程式。該應用程式僅在工作日的工作時間內接收流量。該公司希望根據這種使用情況來優化成本並減少營運開銷。哪種解決方案可以滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company runs an application that uses Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL. The application receives traffic only on weekdays during business hours. The company wants to optimize costs and reduce operational overhead based on this usage. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 使用 AWS 上的執行個體計畫程式設定啟動和停止計畫。
A.en: Use the Instance Scheduler on AWS to configure start and stop schedules.

B.zh: 關閉自動備份。建立資料庫的每週手動快照。
B.en: Turn off automatic backups. Create weekly manual snapshots of the database.

C.zh: 建立自訂 AWS Lambda 函數以根據最低 CPU 使用率啟動和停止資料庫。
C.en: Create a custom AWS Lambda function to start and stop the database based on minimum CPU utilization.

D.zh: 購買所有預先預留的資料庫實例。
D.en: Purchase All Upfront reserved DB instances.

A.zh: 正確。Instance Scheduler 是一種 AWS 解決方案，用於根據預先定義的計劃啟動和停止資源（例如 RDS 實例）。非常適合只需要在工作日和工作時間運行的場景，可以顯著降低成本。
A.en: Correct. Instance Scheduler is an AWS solution for starting and stopping resources (such as RDS instances) based on a predefined schedule. It is ideally suited for scenarios that only need to run during working days and working hours, which can significantly reduce costs.

B.zh: 錯誤。關閉自動備份可能會增加資料遺失的風險。手動建立快照會增加操作開銷，無法滿足降低操作開銷的要求。另外，這種方條並不能直接優化營運成本。
B.en: Incorrect. Turning off automatic backup may increase the risk of data loss. Manually creating snapshots increases operational overhead and does not meet the requirements of reducing operational overhead. In addition, this kind of square bar does not directly optimize operating costs.

C.zh: 錯誤。雖然Lambda函數可以自動化，但根據CPU利用率啟動和停止資料庫並不能滿足問題中「工作日和工作時間」的明確要求。此外，開發和維護自訂Lambda函數會增加操作複雜性，無法達到降低操作開銷的目標。
C.en: Incorrect. Although Lambda functions can be automated, starting and stopping the database based on CPU utilization does not meet the explicit requirements of "working days and working hours" in the question. In addition, developing and maintaining custom Lambda functions increases operational complexity and does not meet the goal of reducing operational overhead.

D.zh: 錯誤。雖然購買 All Upfront 預留實例可以降低長期成本，但不適合這種間歇性的使用情境。預留實例需要全年運行以最大限度地提高成本效益，並且問題明確指出資料庫僅在工作日和工作時間運行。
D.en: Incorrect. Although purchasing All Upfront reserved instances can reduce long-term costs, it is not suitable for this intermittent usage scenario. Reserved instances need to run year-round to maximize cost effectiveness, and the question clearly states that the database only runs on weekdays and during business hours.

answer_text.zh: A. 使用 AWS 上的執行個體計畫程式設定啟動和停止計畫。
answer_text.en: A. Use the Instance Scheduler on AWS to configure start and stop schedules.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (96%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：Instance Scheduler 是一種 AWS 解決方案，用於根據預先定義的計劃啟動和停止資源（例如 RDS 實例）。非常適合只需要在工作日和工作時間運行的場景，可以顯著降低成本。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (96%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Instance Scheduler is an AWS solution for starting and stopping resources (such as RDS instances) based on a predefined schedule. It is ideally suited for scenarios that only need to run during working days and working hours, which can significantly reduce costs.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司使用本地連接儲存在本地運行延遲敏感的應用程式。該公司正在使用直接遷移方法將應用程式遷移到 AWS 雲端。該公司不想改變應用程式架構。哪一種解決方案能夠最具成本效益地滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company uses locally attached storage to run a latency-sensitive application on premises. The company is using a lift and shift method to move the application to the AWS Cloud. The company does not want to change the application architecture. Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 使用 Amazon EC2 執行個體設定 Auto Scaling 群組。使用 Amazon FSx for Lustre 檔案系統執行該應用程式。
A.en: Configure an Auto Scaling group with an Amazon EC2 instance. Use an Amazon FSx for Lustre file system to run the application.

B.zh: 在 Amazon EC2 執行個體上託管應用程式。使用 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) GP2 磁碟區來執行應用程式。
B.en: Host the application on an Amazon EC2 instance. Use an Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) GP2 volume to run the application.

C.zh: 使用 Amazon EC2 執行個體設定 Auto Scaling 群組。使用 Amazon FSx for OpenZFS 檔案系統執行該應用程式。
C.en: Configure an Auto Scaling group with an Amazon EC2 instance. Use an Amazon FSx for OpenZFS file system to run the application.

D.zh: 在 Amazon EC2 執行個體上託管應用程式。使用 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) GP3 磁碟區來執行應用程式。
D.en: Host the application on an Amazon EC2 instance. Use an Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) GP3 volume to run the application.

A.zh: 錯誤。Amazon FSx for Lustre 是專為高效能運算和機器學習而設計的檔案系統，適用於需要高吞吐量的場景。這個問題需要低延遲儲存而不是高吞吐量，因此 FSx for Lustre 不適合這種場景。另外，使用FSx檔案系統可能會增加成本，不符合「最具成本效益」的要求。錯誤原因：不符合低時延、高性價比的要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Amazon FSx for Luster is a file system designed for high-performance computing and machine learning, and is suitable for scenarios that require high throughput. This question requires low-latency storage rather than high throughput, so FSx for Luster is not suitable for this scenario. In addition, using FSx file systems may increase costs and does not meet the "most cost-effective" requirement. Reason for error: Does not meet the requirements of low latency and cost-effectiveness.

B.zh: 錯誤。Amazon EBS GP2 磁碟區提供適合運行應用程式的低延遲儲存。然而，GP3磁碟區是GP2的升級版本，提供更高的效能和更低的成本。錯誤原因：GP2滿足低延遲需求，而GP3更具成本效益。
B.en: Incorrect. Amazon EBS GP2 volumes provide low-latency storage suitable for running applications. However, GP3 volumes are an upgraded version of GP2, offering higher performance and lower cost. Reason for error: While GP2 meets low latency needs, GP3 is more cost-effective.

C.zh: 錯誤。Amazon FSx for OpenZFS 專為檔案儲存而設計，適用於需要檔案共用的場景。這個問題需要區塊儲存來滿足低延遲的要求，而不是檔案儲存。此外，FSx 檔案系統的成本通常高於 EBS 磁碟區。錯誤原因：不符合低時延、高性價比的要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Amazon FSx for OpenZFS is specially designed for file storage and is suitable for scenarios that require file sharing. This question requires block storage to meet low latency requirements, not file storage. Additionally, FSx file systems typically cost more than EBS volumes. Reason for error: Does not meet the requirements of low latency and cost-effectiveness.

D.zh: 正確。Amazon EBS GP3 卷是 GP2 的升級版本，提供更高的效能（固定 IOPS 和吞吐量）和更低的成本。 GP3卷完全滿足低延遲儲存需求，同時滿足「最具成本效益」的要求。是滿足低時延、高性價比要求的最佳選擇。
D.en: Correct. Amazon EBS GP3 volumes are an upgraded version of GP2, providing higher performance (fixed IOPS and throughput) and lower cost. GP3 volumes fully meet low-latency storage needs while meeting the "most cost-effective" requirement. It is the best choice to meet the requirements of low latency and cost-effectiveness.

answer_text.zh: D. 在 Amazon EC2 執行個體上託管應用程式。使用 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) GP3 磁碟區來執行應用程式。
answer_text.en: D. Host the application on an Amazon EC2 instance. Use an Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) GP3 volume to run the application.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：D (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：Amazon EBS GP3 卷是 GP2 的升級版本，提供更高的效能（固定 IOPS 和吞吐量）和更低的成本。 GP3卷完全滿足低延遲儲存需求，同時滿足「最具成本效益」的要求。是滿足低時延、高性價比要求的最佳選擇。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Amazon EBS GP3 volumes are an upgraded version of GP2, providing higher performance (fixed IOPS and throughput) and lower cost. GP3 volumes fully meet low-latency storage needs while meeting the "most cost-effective" requirement. It is the best choice to meet the requirements of low latency and cost-effectiveness.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在 Amazon EC2 執行個體上執行有狀態生產應用程式。該應用程式需要至少兩個 EC2 執行個體才能始終執行。解決方案架構師需要為應用程式設計一個高度可用且容錯的架構。解決方案架構師建立 EC2 執行個體的 Auto Scaling 群組。解決方案架構師應該採取哪一組附加步驟來滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company runs a stateful production application on Amazon EC2 instances. The application requires at least two EC2 instances to always be running. A solutions architect needs to design a highly available and fault-tolerant architecture for the application. The solutions architect creates an Auto Scaling group of EC2 instances. Which set of additional steps should the solutions architect take to meet these requirements?

A.zh: 將 Auto Scaling 組的最小容量設定為 2。在一個可用區中部署一個按需實例，在第二個可用區中部署一個按需實例。
A.en: Set the Auto Scaling group's minimum capacity to two. Deploy one On-Demand Instance in one Availability Zone and one On-Demand Instance in a second Availability Zone.

B.zh: 將 Auto Scaling 組的最小容量設為 4。在一個可用區中部署兩個按需實例，在第二個可用區中部署兩個按需實例。
B.en: Set the Auto Scaling group's minimum capacity to four. Deploy two On-Demand Instances in one Availability Zone and two On-Demand Instances in a second Availability Zone.

C.zh: 將 Auto Scaling 組的最小容量設定為 2。在一個可用區中部署四個 Spot 實例。
C.en: Set the Auto Scaling group's minimum capacity to two. Deploy four Spot Instances in one Availability Zone.

D.zh: 將 Auto Scaling 組的最小容量設為 4。在一個可用區中部署兩個按需實例，在第二個可用區中部署兩個 Spot 實例。
D.en: Set the Auto Scaling group's minimum capacity to four. Deploy two On-Demand Instances in one Availability Zone and two Spot Instances in a second Availability Zone.

A.zh: 錯誤。效能分析：將Auto Scaling組的最小容量設為2是正確的，因為問題需要至少執行兩個實例。儘管將執行個體分佈在兩個可用區是實現高可用性的最佳實踐，但此選項並未明確說明如何處理故障轉移情況。如果某個可用區發生故障，則只會執行一個實例，無法滿足「至少兩個實例運作」的要求。免責聲明：此選項不能完全滿足高可用性和容錯要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Performance analysis: Setting the minimum capacity of the Auto Scaling group to 2 is correct because the question requires at least two instances to be running. Although spreading instances across two Availability Zones is a best practice for high availability, this option does not clearly state how to handle failover situations. If an availability zone fails, only one instance will run, failing to meet the requirement of "at least two instances running". Disclaimer: This option does not fully meet high availability and fault tolerance requirements.

B.zh: 正確。效能分析：將Auto Scaling群組的最小容量設為4，並跨兩個可用區部署實例，確保即使一個可用區出現故障，至少有兩個實例仍在運作。此選項完全滿足高可用性和容錯要求。正確答案。
B.en: Correct. Performance analysis: Set the minimum capacity of the Auto Scaling group to 4, and deploy instances across two availability zones to ensure that even if one availability zone fails, at least two instances are still running. This option fully meets high availability and fault tolerance requirements. the correct answer.

C.zh: 錯誤。效能分析：Spot 執行個體價格便宜，但不適合在生產環境中執行關鍵任務，因為它們可能隨時中斷。僅位於一個可用區中間的實例無法滿足高可用性要求，且使用 Spot 實例不適合關鍵任務的生產環境。不正確。
C.en: Incorrect. Performance analysis: Spot instances are cheap, but are not suitable for running critical tasks in production environments because they can be interrupted at any time. Only instances in the middle of one availability zone cannot meet high availability requirements, and using Spot instances is not suitable for mission-critical production environments. incorrect.

D.zh: 錯誤。效能分析：跨兩個可用區分佈實例是實現高可用性的最佳實踐。使用競價型實例可能會導致執行個體隨時中斷，無法保證生產環境的穩定性。免責聲明：此選項不能完全滿足高可用性和容錯要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Performance analysis: Spreading instances across two availability zones is a best practice for high availability. Using Spot instances may cause the instance to be interrupted at any time, and the stability of the production environment cannot be guaranteed. Disclaimer: This option does not fully meet high availability and fault tolerance requirements.

answer_text.zh: B. 將 Auto Scaling 組的最小容量設為 4。在一個可用區中部署兩個按需實例，在第二個可用區中部署兩個按需實例。
answer_text.en: B. Set the Auto Scaling group's minimum capacity to four. Deploy two On-Demand Instances in one Availability Zone and two On-Demand Instances in a second Availability Zone.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (76%)； A (24%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：效能分析：將Auto Scaling群組的最小容量設為4，並跨兩個可用區部署實例，確保即使一個可用區出現故障，至少有兩個實例仍在運作。此選項完全滿足高可用性和容錯要求。正確答案。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (76%); A (24%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Performance analysis: Set the minimum capacity of the Auto Scaling group to 4, and deploy instances across two availability zones to ensure that even if one availability zone fails, at least two instances are still running. This option fully meets high availability and fault tolerance requirements. the correct answer.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家電子商務公司使用 Amazon Route 53 作為其 DNS 提供者。該公司在本地和 AWS 雲端中託管其網站。該公司的本地資料中心位於 us-west-1 區域附近。該公司使用 eu-central-1 區域來託管該網站。該公司希望盡可能減少網站的載入時間。哪種解決方案可以滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: An ecommerce company uses Amazon Route 53 as its DNS provider. The company hosts its website on premises and in the AWS Cloud. The company's on-premises data center is near the us-west-1 Region. The company uses the eu-central-1 Region to host the website. The company wants to minimize load time for the website as much as possible. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 設定地理位置路由策略。將us-west-1附近的流量傳送到本地資料中心。將eu-central-1附近的流量傳送到eu-central-1。
A.en: Set up a geolocation routing policy. Send the traffic that is near us-west-1 to the on-premises data center. Send the traffic that is near eu-central-1 to eu-central-1.

B.zh: 設定一個簡單的路由策略，將 eu-central-1 附近的所有流量路由到 eu-central-1，並將本地資料中心附近的所有流量路由到本地資料中心。
B.en: Set up a simple routing policy that routes all traffic that is near eu-central-1 to eu-central-1 and routes all traffic that is near the on-premises datacenter to the on-premises data center.

C.zh: 設定延遲路由策略。將該策略與 us-west-1 關聯。
C.en: Set up a latency routing policy. Associate the policy with us-west-1.

D.zh: 設定加權路由策略。在 eu-central-1 和本地資料中心之間均勻分配流量。
D.en: Set up a weighted routing policy. Split the traffic evenly between eu-central-1 and the on-premises data center.

A.zh: 正確。地理位置路由策略允許根據使用者的地理位置將流量路由到最近的資源，從而減少載入時間。此選項滿足問題的要求，因為它會根據使用者的位置將流量分配到最近的資源（us-west-1附近的流量到本地資料中心，eu-central-1附近的流量到AWS雲端）。
A.en: Correct. Geolocation routing policies allow traffic to be routed to the closest resource based on the user's geographic location, thereby reducing load times. this option meets the requirements of the question because it allocates traffic to the nearest resource based on the user's location (traffic near us-west-1 to the local data center, traffic near eu-central-1 to the AWS cloud).

B.zh: 錯誤。簡單路由策略不支援基於使用者位置的流量分配。它只能將流量隨機分配到多個資源或單一資源。此選項所描述的功能實際上是地理位置路由策略的功能，而不是簡單的路由策略。
B.en: Incorrect. Simple routing policy does not support traffic distribution based on user location. It can only randomly distribute traffic to multiple resources or a single resource. The functionality described by this option is actually a functionality of a geo-location routing policy rather than a simple routing policy.

C.zh: 錯誤。路由策略根據資源的網路位置而不是使用者的地理位置來分配流量。問題應該是根據用戶位置來優化流量，而不是根據位置來優化流量，因為這個選項不符合要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Routing policies allocate traffic based on the resource's network location rather than the user's geographic location. The question should optimize traffic based on user location, rather than based on location, because this option does not meet the requirements.

D.zh: 錯誤。加權路由策略根據預先定義的權重而不是使用者的地理位置來分配流量。均勻分佈流量可能會導致使用者存取遠離其地理位置的資源，從而增加載入時間。
D.en: Incorrect. Weighted routing policies distribute traffic based on predefined weights rather than the user's geographic location. Spreading traffic evenly can cause users to access resources far away from their geographical location, increasing load times.

answer_text.zh: A. 設定地理位置路由策略。將us-west-1附近的流量傳送到本地資料中心。將eu-central-1附近的流量傳送到eu-central-1。
answer_text.en: A. Set up a geolocation routing policy. Send the traffic that is near us-west-1 to the on-premises data center. Send the traffic that is near eu-central-1 to eu-central-1.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (89%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：地理位置路由策略允許根據使用者的地理位置將流量路由到最近的資源，從而減少載入時間。此選項滿足問題的要求，因為它會根據使用者的位置將流量分配到最近的資源（us-west-1附近的流量到本地資料中心，eu-central-1附近的流量到AWS雲端）。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (89%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Geolocation routing policies allow traffic to be routed to the closest resource based on the user's geographic location, thereby reducing load times. this option meets the requirements of the question because it allocates traffic to the nearest resource based on the user's location (traffic near us-west-1 to the local data center, traffic near eu-central-1 to the AWS cloud).

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在實體磁帶上擁有 5 PB 的存檔資料。出於合規目的，該公司需要將磁帶上的資料再保存 10 年。該公司希望在未來 6 個月內遷移到 AWS。儲存磁帶的資料中心具有 1 Gbps 上行鏈路網路連線。哪一種解決方案能夠最具成本效益地滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company has 5 PB of archived data on physical tapes. The company needs to preserve the data on the tapes for another 10 years for compliance purposes. The company wants to migrate to AWS in the next 6 months. The data center that stores the tapes has a 1 Gbps uplink internet connectivity. Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 從本地磁帶讀取資料。將資料暫存在本地 NFS 儲存中。使用 AWS DataSync 將資料移轉到 Amazon S3 Glacier 靈活檢索。
A.en: Read the data from the tapes on premises. Stage the data in a local NFS storage. Use AWS DataSync to migrate the data to Amazon S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval.

B.zh: 使用本機備份應用程式從磁帶讀取資料並直接寫入 Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive。
B.en: Use an on-premises backup application to read the data from the tapes and to write directly to Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive.

C.zh: 訂購多個具有磁帶閘道的 AWS Snowball 裝置。將實體磁帶複製到 Snowball 中的虛擬磁帶。將 Snowball 裝置運送到 AWS。建立生命週期策略以將磁帶移至 Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive。
C.en: Order multiple AWS Snowball devices that have Tape Gateway. Copy the physical tapes to virtual tapes in Snowball. Ship the Snowball devices to AWS. Create a lifecycle policy to move the tapes to Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive.

D.zh: 設定本地磁帶閘道。在 AWS 雲端建立虛擬磁帶。使用備份軟體將實體磁帶複製到虛擬磁帶。
D.en: Configure an on-premises Tape Gateway. Create virtual tapes in the AWS Cloud. Use backup software to copy the physical tape to the virtual tape.

A.zh: 錯誤。效能分析：AWS DataSync適合遷移檔案數據，但不直接支援磁帶資料的遷移。磁帶資料需要先轉換為檔案格式，這增加了複雜性。成本分析：使用1Gbps網路連接遷移5PB數據，時間較長，成本較高。缺點：此選項無效且成本高昂。
A.en: Incorrect. Performance analysis: AWS DataSync is suitable for migrating file data, but does not directly support the migration of tape data. The tape data needs to be converted to a file format first, which adds complexity. Cost analysis: Migrating 5 PB of data using a 1 Gbps network connection takes a long time and the cost is relatively high. Cons: This option is ineffective and costly.

B.zh: 錯誤。效能分析：此選項依賴於本機備份應用程序，但沒有提及如何處理磁帶資料的轉換。成本分析：還需要1Gbps的資料遷移，費時又費錢。缺點：此選項無效且成本高昂。
B.en: Incorrect. Performance analysis: This option relies on local backup application procedures, but does not mention how to deal with the conversion of tape data. Cost analysis: It also requires 1 Gbps to migrate data, which is time-consuming and expensive. Cons: This option is ineffective and costly.

C.zh: 正確。效能分析：AWS Snowball 裝置支援磁帶網關，可有效地將實體磁帶轉換為虛擬磁帶並遷移到 AWS。成本分析：使用Snowball設備避免了傳輸的限制，遷移速度快，成本低。最具成本效益的類固醇解決方案。
C.en: Correct. Performance analysis: AWS Snowball devices support Tape Gateway, which can effectively convert physical tapes into virtual tapes and migrate them to AWS. Cost analysis: Using Snowball equipment avoids the limitations of transmission, making migration fast and low-cost. the most cost-effective steroid solution.

D.zh: 錯誤。效能分析：此選項需要 1 Gbps 連線才能將資料移轉到 AWS，這既耗時又昂貴。成本分析：由於傳輸成本比較高，所以最好使用Snowball設備進行遷移。缺點：此選項無效且成本高昂。
D.en: Incorrect. Performance analysis: This option requires a 1 Gbps connection to migrate the data to AWS, which is time-consuming and costly. Cost analysis: Since the transmission cost is relatively high, it is better to use Snowball equipment for migration. Cons: This option is ineffective and costly.

answer_text.zh: C. 訂購多個具有磁帶閘道的 AWS Snowball 裝置。將實體磁帶複製到 Snowball 中的虛擬磁帶。將 Snowball 裝置運送到 AWS。建立生命週期策略以將磁帶移至 Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive。
answer_text.en: C. Order multiple AWS Snowball devices that have Tape Gateway. Copy the physical tapes to virtual tapes in Snowball. Ship the Snowball devices to AWS. Create a lifecycle policy to move the tapes to Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：C (98%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：效能分析：AWS Snowball 裝置支援磁帶網關，可有效地將實體磁帶轉換為虛擬磁帶並遷移到 AWS。成本分析：使用Snowball設備避免了傳輸的限制，遷移速度快，成本低。最具成本效益的類固醇解決方案。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (98%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Performance analysis: AWS Snowball devices support Tape Gateway, which can effectively convert physical tapes into virtual tapes and migrate them to AWS. Cost analysis: Using Snowball equipment avoids the limitations of transmission, making migration fast and low-cost. the most cost-effective steroid solution.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在部署一個並行處理大量資料的應用程式。該公司計劃使用 Amazon EC2 執行個體來處理工作負載。網路架構必須可配置，以防止節點群組共享相同的底層硬體。哪種網路解決方案可以滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company is deploying an application that processes large quantities of data in parallel. The company plans to use Amazon EC2 instances for the workload. The network architecture must be configurable to prevent groups of nodes from sharing the same underlying hardware. Which networking solution meets these requirements?

A.zh: 在分散式置放群組中執行 EC2 執行個體。
A.en: Run the EC2 instances in a spread placement group.

B.zh: 將 EC2 執行個體分組到單獨的帳戶。
B.en: Group the EC2 instances in separate accounts.

C.zh: 使用專用租賃來配置 EC2 執行個體。
C.en: Configure the EC2 instances with dedicated tenancy.

D.zh: 使用共用租賃配置 EC2 執行個體。
D.en: Configure the EC2 instances with shared tenancy.

A.zh: 正確。。分散置放群組旨在將實例分佈在不同的底層硬體上，以最大程度地減少單點故障的影響。這完全滿足了問題的要求，即防止節點共享相同的底層硬體。
A.en: Correct. . Spread Placement Groups are designed to distribute instances across different underlying hardware to minimize the impact of single points of failure. This fully meets the requirements of the question, which is to prevent nodes from sharing the same underlying hardware.

B.zh: 錯誤。。將實例分組到不同的帳戶中並不能保證它們不會共用相同的底層硬體。 AWS帳戶隔離主要用於管理權限和計費，而不是分離底層硬體。
B.en: Incorrect. . Grouping instances into different accounts does not guarantee that they won't share the same underlying hardware. AWS account isolation is primarily used to manage permissions and billing, not to separate the underlying hardware.

C.zh: 錯誤。。專用租賃可確保實例在專用硬體上運行，但並不能阻止多個實例共享相同的底層硬體。它的主要用途是滿足合規性或安全性要求，而不是實現節點分離。
C.en: Incorrect. . Dedicated Tenancy ensures that instances run on dedicated hardware, but it does not prevent multiple instances from sharing the same underlying hardware. Its primary use is to meet compliance or security requirements, not to achieve node separation.

D.zh: 錯誤。。共享租賃是 EC2 執行個體的預設選項，它可以共享底層硬體。這違背了問題中防止共享底層硬體的要求。
D.en: Incorrect. . Shared Tenancy is the default option for EC2 instances, which may share underlying hardware. This goes against the requirement of the question to prevent sharing of underlying hardware.

answer_text.zh: A. 在分散式置放群組中執行 EC2 執行個體。
answer_text.en: A. Run the EC2 instances in a spread placement group.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (87%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：。分散置放群組旨在將實例分佈在不同的底層硬體上，以最大程度地減少單點故障的影響。這完全滿足了問題的要求，即防止節點共享相同的底層硬體。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (87%). Popular discussion and key point summary: . Spread Placement Groups are designed to distribute instances across different underlying hardware to minimize the impact of single points of failure. This fully meets the requirements of the question, which is to prevent nodes from sharing the same underlying hardware.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 解決方案架構師正在設計災難復原 (DR) 策略，以在故障轉移 AWS 區域中提供 Amazon EC2 容量。業務需求顯示災難復原策略必須滿足故障轉移區域的容量。哪種解決方案可以滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A solutions architect is designing a disaster recovery (DR) strategy to provide Amazon EC2 capacity in a failover AWS Region. Business requirements state that the DR strategy must meet capacity in the failover Region. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 在故障轉移區域中購買按需實例。
A.en: Purchase On-Demand Instances in the failover Region.

B.zh: 在故障轉移區域購買 EC2 Savings Plan。
B.en: Purchase an EC2 Savings Plan in the failover Region.

C.zh: 在故障轉移區域中購買區域預留實例。
C.en: Purchase regional Reserved Instances in the failover Region.

D.zh: 在故障轉移區域購買容量預留。
D.en: Purchase a Capacity Reservation in the failover Region.

A.zh: 錯誤。按需實例是按需購買的實例。雖然它們可以快速啟動，但不能保證在故障轉移期間有足夠的可用容量，因為容量可能會受到其他用戶競爭的影響。
A.en: Incorrect. On-Demand Instances are instances purchased on demand. Although they can be started quickly, there is no guarantee that sufficient capacity will be available during failover because the capacity may be affected by competition from other users.

B.zh: 錯誤。EC2 Savings Plan 主要用於最佳化成本而非保證容量。它不能確保故障轉移區域中有足夠的計算資源可用。
B.en: Incorrect. EC2 Savings Plan is mainly used to optimize costs rather than guarantee capacity. It does not ensure that sufficient computing resources are available in the failover area.

C.zh: 錯誤。預留實例雖然可以提供成本最佳化和容量預留，但其容量預留是基於實例類型和區域的長期承諾，無法靈活滿足災難復原的需求。
C.en: Incorrect. Although Reserved Instances can provide cost optimization and capacity reservation, its capacity reservation is based on long-term commitment of instance type and region and cannot flexibly meet the needs of disaster recovery.

D.zh: 正確。容量預留可讓您在指定區域和可用區預留 EC2 容量，確保在發生故障轉移時有足夠的運算資源可用。這是災難復原場景中最合適的選項。
D.en: Correct. Capacity Reservation allows you to reserve EC2 capacity in specified regions and Availability Zones, ensuring that sufficient computing resources are available in the event of a failover. This is the most appropriate option in disaster recovery scenarios.

answer_text.zh: D. 在故障轉移區域購買容量預留。
answer_text.en: D. Purchase a Capacity Reservation in the failover Region.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：D (93%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：容量預留可讓您在指定區域和可用區預留 EC2 容量，確保在發生故障轉移時有足夠的運算資源可用。這是災難復原場景中最合適的選項。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (93%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Capacity Reservation allows you to reserve EC2 capacity in specified regions and Availability Zones, ensuring that sufficient computing resources are available in the event of a failover. This is the most appropriate option in disaster recovery scenarios.
